     The Numbers Business Report

Premium Rush (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $20,275,446Details
International Box Office $11,117,755
Worldwide Box Office $31,393,201
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $7,222,088
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,127,048
Total $8,349,136
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Dodging speeding cars, crazed cabbies, open doors, and eight million cranky pedestrians is all in a day's work for Wilee, the best of New York's agile and aggressive bicycle messengers. It takes a special breed to ride the fixie - super lightweight, single-gear bikes with no brakes and riders who are equal part skilled cyclists and suicidal nutcases who risk becoming a smear on the pavement every time they head into traffic. But a guy who's used to putting his life on the line is about to get more than even he is used to when a routine delivery turns into a life or death chase through the streets of Manhattan. When Wilee picks up his last envelope of the day on a premium rush run, he discovers this package is different. This time, someone is actually trying to kill him.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$6,030,164 (29.7% of total gross)
Legs:3.36 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:64.6% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$35,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,255 opening theaters/2,255 max. theaters, 4.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $30,464,117

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: August 24th, 2012 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: August 23rd, 2012 (Wide) (Malaysia)
August 23rd, 2012 (Wide) (Curacao)
August 23rd, 2012 (Wide) (Taiwan)
August 30th, 2012 (Wide) (Aruba)
August 30th, 2012 (Wide) (Suriname)
Video Release: December 21st, 2012 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some violence, intense action sequences and language.
(Rating bulletin 2226, 6/6/2012)
Running Time: 91 minutes
Geographical setting: New York, New York City
Plot point: Addiction, Bike Messengers, Corrupt Cops, Delayed Adulthood, Fugitive / On the Run, Gambling, Gangs, Immigration, Non-Chronological
Social setting: Organized Crime
Subgenre: Action Thriller
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Thriller/Suspense
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Columbia Pictures, Pariah Productions
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Dodging speeding cars, crazed cabbies, open doors, and eight million cranky pedestrians is all in a day's work for Wilee, the best of New York's agile and aggressive bicycle messengers. It takes a special breed to ride the fixie - super lightweight, single-gear bikes with no brakes and riders who are equal part skilled cyclists and suicidal nutcases who risk becoming a smear on the pavement every time they head into traffic. But a guy who's used to putting his life on the line is about to get more than even he is used to when a routine delivery turns into a life or death chase through the streets of Manhattan. When Wilee picks up his last envelope of the day on a premium rush run, he discovers this package is different. This time, someone is actually trying to kill him.
